note: This session processed a comprehensive 20-point reliability analysis from user feedback. The analysis identified critical operational gaps that align with the existing Phase 3 direction but highlighted missing infrastructure: safe degraded mode, desktop doctor, event taxonomy enforcement, secrets handling policy, plugin security manifests, trust tiers, and first-run UX depth. The orchestrator created a Phase 4 Reliability plan, launched 5 lane-owners in parallel for deep exploration, completed a baseline assessment of all 7 areas, registered 5 cross-session lessons, and pre-fabricated architect prompts for the next wave. Currently awaiting lane-owner handoffs. Key discovery: the event schema (runtime-event.ts) exists with the right structure but eventType is Schema.String (freeform) — exactly matching the user's point about ad-hoc naming making DuckDB analytics unreliable. The debug-packet.ts already has redactConfig/isSensitiveKey (partially addressing secrets handling) but needs broader coverage. Plugin config has zero capability manifests. Permission model is flat ask/allow/deny — no trust tiers.
